title: GTFOBins Quick Lookup
when_to_use: When SUID or sudo provides escalation paths

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Navigate to gtfobins.github.io in browser for interactive binary lookup
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for gtfobins quick lookup
steps.expected_output: Access to GTFOBins database with searchable interface

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Search for binary name discovered during enumeration phase
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Matching binary entry with available exploitation functions

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Review available functions including Shell, SUID, Sudo, and Capabilities
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: List of exploitation methods applicable to discovered binary

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Select matching function corresponding to exploitation scenario (SUID, Sudo, etc)
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Specific exploitation command sequence for target scenario

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Copy command sequence and adapt parameters for target environment
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Customized exploit command ready for execution

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Execute adapted command sequence to achieve privilege escalation
steps.expected_output: Successful elevation to root or higher privilege user account

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Verify escalation with 'id' command confirming new privilege level
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Confirmed root access with uid=0 and gid=0 or sudo group membership
